title: madhuca sarawakensis
intro: Madhuca sarawakensis, a species of flowering plant in the family Sapotaceae, is native to Sri Lanka and is also known as the "Sri Lankan Butter Tree" due to its high oil content. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Madhuca sarawakensis include reducing fever, relieving pain, and aiding in the treatment of various skin conditions. Herbal preparations of the plant include oil extractions and decoctions, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, including wounds and digestive problems.
